behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names all the specific files, function names (8 framework helpers, 2 workers helpers), test files, and behaviors (expect as parameter, at least 3 validation checks, justified eslint-disable in to-exist.ts) that the tests assert on.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what to change (refactor to accept expect param, remove comments, use throw new Error) without giving exact diffs or line numbers.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for 'expect:' as a parameter (any position), throw new Error count >= 3, and expect as first-positional in call sites — reasonable structural checks that allow alternative implementations.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests run pnpm turbo typecheck, unit tests, build, lint, and format in addition to regex structural checks — actual execution of the code.

test_not_tautological.reason: f2p tests require actual refactoring (param injection, removing imports, replacing expect.fail with throw) — a stub cannot satisfy e.g. the typecheck + param injection + call-site updates together.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors listed in instruction.md (eslint-disable cleanup, param injection for named functions, mocks.ts changes, call site updates, lint/build/tests) have corresponding test functions.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are divided with section comments ('Pass-to-pass', 'Fail-to-pass') and each test has a clear docstring and tag.

no_typos.reason: Filenames and function names are consistent between instruction.md and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names files but that is the task scope; no line numbers or exact code changes are revealed.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code refactor task, not an agentmd/config edit task.
